content: 我们团队正在做 AI 基础设施方向的工作，日常需要系统性评测各类中转站的可靠性，帮助内部项目做选型决策。 市面上的评测大多是"能不能连上"、"速度怎么样"，但对我们来说更关键的问题是：这个接口背后到底在跑什么？有没有在请求或响应上动手脚？ 为了把这件事做系统，我们写了 Probe…

title: [开源] 我正在用 Python 复刻经典游戏红色警戒 2
content: Developer Silicicat has built a Python version of the classic game Red Alert 2 using Pygame. The project has completed terrain, MIX and SHP parsing/rendering and a new UI system, including the sidebar. The focus is on resource loading management, decoupling sidebar animation from core logic, and pixel‑perfect UI recreation. Code is open‑source on GitHub (cookgreen/ChronoStorm) and includes a YouTube tutorial. The goal is to finish the full Python remake and integrate DeepSeek.

title: Balaji and Taylor Lorenz on AI and Media
content: Theo Jaffee hosts a conversation between Balaji Srinivasan and Taylor Lorenz about AI’s impact on media, trust, and online communication. The discussion revisits their previous disagreements, examines the collapse of traditional information systems, and highlights the rise of AI‑generated content. They explore the need for new identity and truth‑verification models and contrast visions such as decentralized “webs of trust,” cryptographic verification, and the future role of journalism, privacy, and accountability.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Theo Jaffee, Balaji Srinivasan, Taylor Lorenz)

normalizedTitle: balaji and taylor lorenz on ai and media

content: The article examines how a potential war in Iran could alter President Trump’s approach to conflict. It questions whether the situation would make him cautious and reluctant to act, or instead drive him to take decisive, possibly reckless military action. The piece analyses Trump’s historical decision‑making patterns and explores how escalating tensions might influence his choice between restraint and aggressive posture.

title: 时隔26年翻红的余乐天，凭啥成为打工人头号嘴替
content: The article explains why the character Yu Letian from the 2000 Hong Kong sitcom “Man in the Middle” remains popular with modern office workers.  His self‑deprecating humor, informal style, and “anti‑boss” attitude turn workplace frustrations into relatable jokes that many use as memes.  Yu’s personality—laid‑back, resigned yet optimistic—reflects the realities of the 1998–2000 financial crisis and the “996” work culture, offering a form of therapeutic self‑parody for today’s laboring class.

title: 贬低股市并非央行行长的职责
content: Central bank leaders, by publicly criticizing falling stock markets, risk stretching beyond their monetary policy mandate. Their remarks can influence investors and markets, creating potential conflicts with their core responsibilities to maintain price stability and support the economy. This maneuver may expand their role inadvertently, leading to mission creep and unintended consequences for financial stability.

title: 新加坡总理警告人工智能可能引发的颠覆性影响，并承诺支持劳动者
content: Singapore’s Prime Minister issued a warning about the disruptive potential of artificial intelligence, citing risks that AI could significantly impact employment and economic stability. He emphasized the government’s commitment to safeguarding workers, promising initiatives and support measures to help those affected by AI-driven changes. The briefing highlighted the need for proactive policies and social protections as the country navigates rapid technological advancements.

content: Japan’s Minister Takaichi addressed business concerns by assuring continued supply of naphtha, a key petrochemical feedstock. He confirmed that Japan would maintain an adequate supply chain for the product, aiming to alleviate fears among companies worried about potential shortages. The statement was made in response to industry anxieties about future availability and is intended to stabilize the domestic market.

content: Huawei’s AI chip sales have increased sharply amid Nvidia’s slowdown in the Chinese market. title: 2026 05 01 HackerNews
content: The Hacker News roundup reports several tech incidents: a Linux kernel privilege‑escalation flaw (CVE‑2026‑31431) that exploits AF_ALG and splice to modify setuid files and elevate local users; a Claude Code billing bug that mis‑charges for commits containing “HERMES.md”; OpenAI’s removal of “goblin” metaphors from Codex after discovering they were over‑used; a “OpenClaw” keyword triggering Claude Code rejection or extra billing; Belgium halting nuclear plant decommissioning in favor of state ownership; the Zig project banning LLM‑assisted contributions to avoid low‑quality PRs; Mozilla opposing Google’s Prompt API over privacy concerns; Meta ending a contract with an outsourcing firm after employees exposed privacy violations in smart‑glasses content review; Meta’s handling of an NSA surveillance exposé by Mark Klein; and Spain’s parliament moving to limit IP blockages imposed by LaLiga for anti‑piracy.
eventKey: 2026-hackernews
normalizedTitle: 2026 05 01 hackernews

title: 白宫反对 Anthropic 扩大 Mythos 模型使用范围的计划
content: The White House opposes Anthropic’s plan to broaden access to its AI model Mythos, which can launch cyberattacks and create widespread network disruption. Anthropic would allow around 70 additional companies and organizations, raising the total number of authorized users to about 120. U.S. officials cited safety concerns and a lack of sufficient computing power that could limit the government’s ability to use the model.

title: 马斯克诉 OpenAI 案法庭证词与推文矛盾，称特斯拉未研发通用人工智能
content: Elon Musk appeared in California federal court to sue Sam Altman and other OpenAI founders, alleging they “stole from a nonprofit” and misrepresented the company’s transition to for‑profit status. Musk claimed that switches in Microsoft’s investment restrictions prompted the lawsuit and insisted that revenue limits fundamentally differ for investors. He also contradicted his own statements about Tesla’s AI projects and faced questions over hiring OpenAI staff. The judge paused some questioning, allowing limited testimony before the hearing continues on Thursday.

title: OpenAI 硬件负责人的闭门分享：硬件的「终点」依旧是智能手机
content: OpenAI is moving into hardware to secure control over AI’s low‑level operations. The company has already tape‑out a chip in two years and plans a smartphone that meets future model demands, rather than just building an agent. By hiring former Jony Ive team members, OpenAI aims to design end‑side devices. Unlike other AI firms, it seeks a full‑stack redefinition, shifting from model provider to infrastructure and device company, with the phone as a natural step toward an agent‑centric computing paradigm.
eventKey: openai-硬件负责人的闭门分享-硬件的「终点」依旧是智能手机

content: On April 30, Xiaohongshu announced a new organizational upgrade, creating two AI-focused departments—Dots and Enterprise Intelligence—to boost AI integration across product, technology, and operations. Concurrently, Conan was appointed president to unify the community, e‑commerce, and commercialization units, reporting directly to CEO Seiya, while Mulan remains chair of the board.

title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Interactive v. non-interactive mode
content: GitHub Copilot CLI offers two modes: interactive and non‑interactive. Interactive mode, activated by simply typing `copilot`, provides a chat‑like session where you can ask questions, review answers, and continue the dialogue within a single session. Non‑interactive mode, invoked with `copilot -p "prompt"`, delivers a quick, single response directly in the shell, ideal for one‑off tasks like summarizing code or generating snippets. Sessions can be resumed with `/resume` or `copilot --resume`.
eventKey: github-copilot-cli-beginners-interactive-non-interactive
normalizedTitle: github copilot cli for beginners interactive v non-interactive mode
